title: jasminum grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Jasminum grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum, also known as sambac or night-blooming jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Jasminum grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum include essential oils, teas, and infusions, which can be used to promote a sense of calm and well-being, and to support overall health and wellness.
